title_en: Ash-Shafi'i: Founder of the School and the Science of Legal Theory
title_ar: الإمام الشافعي: مؤسس المذهب وعلم أصول الفقه
content_en:
The biography of ash-Shafi'i that opens Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra is one of the work's most important sections and one of the richest biographical accounts of any Islamic legal scholar. Ash-Shafi'i was not merely the founder of a legal school but one of the most original thinkers in the history of Islamic scholarship, and as-Subki's biography attempts to convey both his intellectual achievements and his personal qualities.

Muhammad ibn Idris ash-Shafi'i was born in Gaza (or possibly Mecca, according to different accounts) in 150 AH, the year of Abu Hanifa's death. He was raised in Mecca and memorized the Quran as a child. He studied with Malik ibn Anas in Medina, learning the Madinese legal tradition and the Muwatta. He traveled to Iraq, where he encountered the Hanafi tradition and the rationalist legal methodology associated with Abu Hanifa's disciples. He subsequently lived in Yemen, returned to Baghdad, and eventually settled in Egypt, where he died in 204 AH.

This intellectual biography — training under both the great traditions of his time and then synthesizing them in a new framework — is directly relevant to ash-Shafi'i's most significant intellectual contribution: the founding of the science of usul al-fiqh (legal theory or the principles of jurisprudence). His Risalah fi Usul al-Fiqh was the first systematic treatise on the sources of Islamic law and the rules for deriving legal rulings from them. It established the framework — Quran, Sunnah, consensus (ijma'), and analogical reasoning (qiyas) — within which all subsequent Islamic legal theory has operated.

As-Subki's biography discusses ash-Shafi'i's Risalah and its intellectual significance, presenting it as an achievement that transcended any single legal school. Ash-Shafi'i's articulation of usul al-fiqh provided a meta-framework for understanding the diversity of legal opinion within the Sunni tradition — showing how different schools could reach different conclusions from the same sources while all operating within a legitimate methodological framework.

The biography also preserves ash-Shafi'i's famous statements about knowledge, piety, and the proper conduct of scholars — statements that became part of the Shafi'i school's identity and that are cited across the tradition as guides to scholarly virtue.
title_id: Asy-Syafi'i: Pendiri Mazhab dan Ilmu Teori Hukum
content_id:
Biografi asy-Syafi'i yang membuka Thabaqat asy-Syafi'iyyah al-Kubra adalah salah satu bagian terpenting dari karya ini dan salah satu catatan biografi terkaya dari ulama hukum Islam mana pun. Asy-Syafi'i bukan sekadar pendiri mazhab hukum tetapi salah satu pemikir paling orisinal dalam sejarah beasiswa Islam, dan biografi as-Subki mencoba menyampaikan baik pencapaian intelektualnya maupun kualitas pribadinya.

Muhammad ibn Idris asy-Syafi'i lahir di Gaza (atau mungkin Makkah, menurut berbagai catatan) pada tahun 150 H, tahun wafatnya Abu Hanifah. Beliau dibesarkan di Makkah dan menghafal Al-Qur'an sebagai anak-anak. Beliau belajar dengan Malik ibn Anas di Madinah, mempelajari tradisi hukum Madinah dan Muwatha'. Beliau kemudian bepergian ke Irak, di mana beliau menemukan tradisi Hanafi dan metodologi hukum rasionalis yang terkait dengan murid-murid Abu Hanifah. Beliau kemudian tinggal di Yaman, kembali ke Baghdad, dan akhirnya menetap di Mesir, di mana beliau wafat pada tahun 204 H.

Biografi intelektual ini — pelatihan di bawah kedua tradisi besar zamannya dan kemudian mensintesisnya dalam kerangka baru — secara langsung relevan dengan kontribusi intelektual asy-Syafi'i yang paling signifikan: pendirian ilmu usul al-fiqh (teori hukum atau prinsip-prinsip yurisprudensi). Risalah-nya fi Usul al-Fiqh adalah risalah sistematis pertama tentang sumber-sumber hukum Islam dan aturan-aturan untuk menurunkan hukum-hukum darinya. Risalah itu menetapkan kerangka — Al-Qur'an, Sunnah, ijma' (konsensus), dan qiyas (penalaran analogis) — di dalam mana seluruh teori hukum Islam berikutnya telah beroperasi.

Biografi as-Subki membahas Risalah asy-Syafi'i dan signifikansi intelektualnya, menyajikannya sebagai pencapaian yang melampaui mazhab hukum mana pun. Artikulasi asy-Syafi'i tentang usul al-fiqh menyediakan meta-kerangka untuk memahami keragaman pendapat hukum dalam tradisi Sunni — menunjukkan bagaimana mazhab-mazhab berbeda dapat mencapai kesimpulan berbeda dari sumber-sumber yang sama sambil semua beroperasi dalam kerangka metodologis yang sah.

Biografi ini juga melestarikan pernyataan-pernyataan terkenal asy-Syafi'i tentang ilmu, kesalehan, dan perilaku yang tepat dari ulama — pernyataan yang menjadi bagian dari identitas mazhab Syafi'i dan yang dikutip di seluruh tradisi sebagai panduan kebajikan ilmiah.
content_ar: The biography of ash-الشافعي that opens Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra is one of the work's most المهم sections and one of the richest biographical accounts of any Islamic legal scholar. Ash-الشافعي was not merely the founder of a legal school but one of the most original thinkers in the history of Islamic scholarship, and as-Subki's biography attempts to convey both his intellectual achievements and his personal qualities. محمد ﷺ ibn Idris ash-الشافعي was born in Gaza (or possibly مكة المكرمة, وفقاً لـ different accounts) in 150 AH, the year of Abu Hanifa's الموت. He was raised in مكة المكرمة and memorized the القرآن الكريم as a child. He studied with مالك ibn أنس in المدينة المنورة, learning the Madinese legal التراث and the الموطأ. He traveled to Iraq, where he encountered the الحنفي التراث and the rationalist legal methodology associated with Abu Hanifa's disciples. He subsequently lived in Yemen, returned to Baghdad, and eventually settled in Egypt, where he died in 204 AH. This intellectual biography — training under both the great traditions of his time and then synthesizing them in a new framework — is directly relevant to ash-الشافعي's most المهم intellectual contribution: the founding of the science of الأصول al-الفقه (legal theory or the principles of jurisprudence). His Risalah fi الأصول al-الفقه was the أولاً systematic treatise on the sources of Islamic law and the rules for deriving legal rulings from them. It established the framework — القرآن الكريم, السنة, consensus (الإجماع'), and analogical reasoning (القياس) — within which all subsequent Islamic legal theory has operated. As-Subki's biography discusses ash-الشافعي's Risalah and its intellectual significance, presenting it as an achievement that transcended any single legal school. Ash-الشافعي's articulation of الأصول al-الفقه provided a meta-framework for الفهم the diversity of legal opinion within the Sunni التراث — showing how different schools could reach different conclusions from the same sources بينما all operating within a legitimate methodological framework. The biography أيضاً preserves ash-الشافعي's famous statements about العلم, piety, and the proper السلوك of العلماء — statements that became part of the الشافعي school's identity and that are cited across the التراث as guides to العلمي الفضيلة.
